name: Lawyers505.com
description: A modern website refresh for Lawyers505.com (Lawyers 505), a law firm in Albuquerque, New Mexico handling divorce, criminal defense, personal injury, and family law. Rebuild the outdated early-2010s GoDaddy Website Builder site using the original site's content and assets, replacing obsolete elements like dead Google+ links, separate mobile site behavior, fixed/preformatted text layouts, and image-heavy outdated design with a responsive modern experience.
personaStyleGuide: Visual Anchor: monumental editorial typography cut against sun-baked New Mexico restraint. The site should feel authoritative, contemporary, and grounded in Albuquerque rather than corporate-generic. Use oversized serif headlines with sharp sans-serif support text to create a courtroom-meets-editorial rhythm. Favor full-width compositions, asymmetrical splits, and cardless content bands with strong negative space instead of boxed UI. Spacing should feel deliberate and architectural: large vertical breaks, disciplined alignment, and generous margins that let key statements carry weight. Color should draw from desert stone, legal formality, and civic confidence: a deep ink foundation, warm mineral neutrals, and a precise copper-sand accent rather than bright tech colors. Shadows should be minimal to nonexistent; rely on contrast, scale, rules, and layered typography instead of floating components. Imagery should feel grounded, human, and regionally rooted, with restrained treatment so the design is not image-heavy like the legacy site. Avoid ornamental law-firm clichés, glossy stock aesthetics, and dashboard-like cards. Interaction should be smooth and responsive, with fluid layout shifts that eliminate the old separate mobile-site behavior and any fixed or preformatted text feeling.
features: Removal of obsolete elements like dead Google+ links
Unified experience replacing separate mobile site behavior

sections.title: Hero
sections.description: Introduce Lawyers 505 as a law firm in Albuquerque, New Mexico with a confident, modern first impression that reframes the original site's content and assets without the outdated early-2010s presentation.
sections.layoutHint: Full-bleed editorial hero with oversized serif headline, restrained supporting copy, and a strong horizontal composition using one regional or firm-relevant visual anchor instead of multiple stacked boxes.

sections.title: Practice Areas
sections.description: Present the firm's exact service focus clearly: divorce, criminal defense, personal injury, and family law.
sections.layoutHint: Cardless split-layout with large typographic service names, staggered columns, and generous spacing so each practice area reads as a major pillar rather than a small feature tile.

sections.title: Content and Assets Refresh
sections.description: Reuse the original site's content and assets while modernizing their presentation, replacing fixed or preformatted text layouts and reducing the outdated image-heavy feel.
sections.layoutHint: Long-form editorial content bands with adaptive text measure, integrated legacy asset placement, and responsive image-text choreography that feels fluid on all screen sizes.

sections.title: Modernization Cleanup
sections.description: Remove obsolete elements such as dead Google+ links and eliminate separate mobile site behavior in favor of one responsive experience across devices.
sections.layoutHint: Utility-driven footer or lower-page system area with clean inline navigation patterns, modern responsive structure, and no legacy-era widgets or duplicated mobile-only treatments.

branding.aesthetic: Southwestern editorial minimalism with legal gravitas

imagery.style: Cinematic Southwestern legal editorial photography with restrained contrast, architectural framing, and grounded human presence
